title: Establish v18 contract foundation and Oracle/APR boundary invariants
description:
## Background
Contract and boundary epic. The v18 plan is contract-first because Oracle, APR, and vibe-planning may be implemented independently. Oracle must carry enough schemas, fixtures, and metadata to prove its browser/provider mechanics while keeping planning policy outside Oracle.

## Design Notes
Treat required schema fields as strict contract core and unknown fields as non-gating extensions. Any critical eligibility decision must be based on core fields only. Keep API-substitution bans, mode verification, verified-before-submit evidence, formal-first-plan status, and synthesis eligibility from being overridden by extension fields.

## Success Criteria
Contracts and validators are represented in repo code and CI; Oracle exposes provider access metadata without taking over APR policy; stale provider docs and stale bundle assumptions are surfaced before live calls. Unit tests and contract fixture smoke checks cover required fields, extension-field non-override behavior, negative fixtures, and provider access restrictions.

## Future-Self Notes
This epic exists to prevent late integration drift. If future agents change a shared contract, they must update schemas, fixtures, robot docs, tests, and references across all three bundles or centralize the contract package in a later integration pass.
design: Treat required schema fields as strict contract core and unknown fields as non-gating extensions. Any critical eligibility decision must be based on core fields only. Keep API-substitution bans, mode verification, verified-before-submit evidence, formal-first-plan status, and synthesis eligibility from being overridden by extension fields.
acceptance_criteria: Contracts and validators are represented in repo code and CI; unit/fixture tests cover strict-core fields, extension-field non-override behavior, negative fixtures, provider access restrictions, and stale provider docs.
notes: Contract changes must update schemas, fixtures, robot docs, tests, and references across all three bundles or centralize the contract package later.

title: Build browser evidence object creation and hash provenance
description:
## Background
Create a browser evidence builder for browser_evidence.v1 required fields: schema_version, bundle_version, evidence_id, provider_slot, provider, requested_mode, mode_verified, verified_before_prompt_submit, verified_at, prompt_submitted_at, verification_method, capture_confidence, redaction_policy, session_id_hash, selector_manifest_version, transition_log_sha256, prompt_sha256, output_text_sha256, unsafe_artifacts_quarantined, created_at, run_id, provider_result_id, verification_scope, requested_reasoning_effort, observed_reasoning_effort_label, reasoning_effort_verified, effort_rank, selected_effort_is_highest_visible, available_effort_labels_hash, and browser_effort_strategy.

## Design Notes
Centralize hashing so SHA-256 values are full sha256:<64 hex> strings. Verify timestamp ordering and block success if verification happens after prompt submission or if required fields are missing.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover success evidence for ChatGPT and Gemini, unverified evidence rejection, placeholder hash rejection, timestamp ordering, and prompt/output byte hashing.

## Future-Self Notes
This is the core proof artifact APR will trust.

title: Implement ChatGPT and Gemini provider-specific doctor surfaces
description:
## Background
Add provider-specific doctors: oracle chatgpt doctor --pro --extended-reasoning --remote-browser preferred --json and oracle gemini doctor --deep-think --remote-browser preferred --json. These verify readiness and, when safe, same-session UI mode/effort observability without submitting user prompts.

## Design Notes
Doctors should report login state, remote/local browser session state, selector manifest version, observed labels/rank where verification is possible, evidence requirement, lease status, and typed blockers. They must not silently downgrade to lower modes.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover login required, UI drift suspected, unavailable remote browser, verified mode, and missing effort control cases for both providers.

## Future-Self Notes
Provider doctors are the bridge between generic capabilities and actual protected browser route readiness.

title: Create shared json_envelope.v1 helpers and error taxonomy
description:
## Background
Implement a shared JSON envelope builder for robot-facing commands with ok, schema_version, data, meta, warnings, errors, commands, blocked_reason, next_command, fix_command, and retry_safe. Add named error codes required by v18.

## Design Notes
Required errors include provider_login_required, browser_lock_timeout, remote_browser_unavailable, remote_browser_auth_failed, chatgpt_pro_unverified, chatgpt_extended_reasoning_unverified, gemini_deep_think_unverified, ui_drift_suspected, output_capture_empty, output_capture_unverified, provider_usage_limit, prompt_submitted_before_verification, and remote_browser_token_missing.

## Acceptance Criteria
All new robot commands use the helper; tests verify failure envelopes include recovery fields and do not require parsing prose.

## Future-Self Notes
This is the base dependency for most CLI work. Keep it small and idiomatic to the existing CLI code.

title: Test lease concurrency, stale locks, and provider isolation
description:
## Background
Add focused concurrency tests proving provider-scoped locks serialize same-provider browser runs while allowing independent provider locks when safe. Validate stale locks and recovery paths.

## Design Notes
Use local fixture or fake browser executors rather than live providers. Include ChatGPT+ChatGPT contention, Gemini+Gemini contention, ChatGPT+Gemini separate locks, expired TTL, corrupt lock file, and recover command cases.

## Acceptance Criteria
The tests are deterministic in CI and never require real Chrome or provider credentials.

## Future-Self Notes
This directly prevents FM-001/FM-002 cross-run contamination.

title: Build end-to-end mock route rehearsal for Oracle surfaces
description:
## Background
Create an E2E mock rehearsal that exercises the Oracle-owned surfaces without live providers: capabilities, doctor, remote doctor, browser leases plan/acquire/release/recover, ChatGPT doctor, Gemini doctor, evidence verify, and a mocked provider result.

## Design Notes
The rehearsal should prove JSON envelopes, command sequencing, recovery fields, leases, evidence, and provider result linkage all work together before live cutover. It must use the shared v18 structured logging standard: every step logs the command, provider slot, mock/live flag, lease/evidence IDs, elapsed time, sanitized stdout/stderr path, and next/fix command when blocked.

## Acceptance Criteria
A single pnpm/vitest or script command runs in CI and produces actionable failure output plus a sanitized artifact packet. The packet includes per-step JSON envelopes, evidence verification output, a redaction assertion summary, and enough timing information to distinguish schema failure from remote/browser waiting.

## Future-Self Notes
This is Oracle's portion of the v18 mock route rehearsal before integration replaces mocks with live calls. It should be the first high-signal E2E command a future agent runs after unit tests.

title: Build fixture, unit, browser, and live validation coverage
description:
## Background
Validation epic. The plan requires comprehensive unit tests, fixture tests, browser fixture tests, and opt-in live smokes because mocks alone are not release readiness.

## Design Notes
Keep live provider tests opt-in and aligned with AGENTS.md: OpenAI live tests use ORACLE_LIVE_TEST=1; Pro browser runs can take up to 10 minutes or longer; never auto-click Answer now; browser smokes must preserve Markdown and inspect DOM/session artifacts when output looks echoed or flattened.

## Success Criteria
CI-safe tests cover contracts, errors, leases, evidence, redaction, selectors, output capture, remote token redaction, and mock route rehearsal. Live smoke beads remain gated and documented.

## Future-Self Notes
This epic is the proof layer that converts the plan from narrative to enforceable behavior.

title: Implement browser lease storage and TTL model
description:
## Background
Create the storage model for synchronous browser leases, likely under ~/.oracle/browser-leases, with one lock per provider over the shared logical profile. Each lock records lease_id, provider, profile_id_hash, local pid or remote session id, acquired/issued time, expires_at, ttl_seconds, holder, command summary, status, and safe recovery command.

## Design Notes
Do not silently break stale locks. Detect stale/expired/active states and surface them with recover commands. Keep the design cross-platform enough for macOS, Linux, and Windows path conventions already used by Oracle.

## Acceptance Criteria
Unit tests cover create/read/renew/expire/stale detection, corrupt lock files, profile hash mismatch, and lock metadata redaction.

## Future-Self Notes
This is the storage primitive for the lease commands and provider runs.

title: Maintain source baseline, source trust, and artifact-index hash compatibility
description:
## Background
Where Oracle emits provider results or evidence, preserve source_baseline_sha256, source_trust references, artifact index links, and traceability-compatible IDs passed from APR/vibe-planning. Oracle should not own source trust policy, but it must not corrupt or invent these correlation handles.

## Design Notes
Handle source-baseline policy vs legacy mode compatibility when present: if both appear they must agree. Unknown artifact fields are extensions and cannot override evidence or synthesis eligibility.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover hash pass-through, policy/mode agreement, artifact index linking to evidence/result paths, and placeholder sha256 rejection.

## Future-Self Notes
This keeps Oracle artifacts joinable with APR's Plan IR and human review packet.

title: Materialize v18 JSON schemas as TypeScript contracts and validators
description:
## Background
Bring the v18 contract core into Oracle implementation as typed TypeScript shapes and runtime validators where Oracle emits or consumes those artifacts: json-envelope, provider capability, browser lease, browser evidence, browser session, remote browser endpoint, route readiness metadata, provider result, robot surface, run progress, and context serialization policy passthrough.

## Design Notes
Use the plan schemas as the field-level source. Keep additionalProperties-style forward compatibility, but enforce all required core fields on emitted Oracle artifacts. Prefer existing repo validation patterns and zod or local type guards if already present.

## Acceptance Criteria
Unit tests prove required fields are present, schema_version values are correct, unknown extension fields do not override core fields, and emitted examples match the v18 fixtures.

## Future-Self Notes
Oracle does not need to own APR-only artifacts deeply, but it must emit compatible metadata and pass through hashes without corrupting them.

title: Prepare integration, documentation, changelog, and release handoff
description:
## Background
Handoff epic. After implementation, Oracle must be easy to integrate with APR and vibe-planning and easy for future agents to validate without opening the original v18 bundle.

## Design Notes
Produce contract hash artifacts, evidence samples, live cutover support, manual smoke updates, README/ROBOTS updates, and a grouped changelog entry for end-user-visible behavior. Do not ship balanced/audit claims until the integration pass can replace mocks route by route.

## Success Criteria
Docs and release gates explain what changed, how to validate it, what remains opt-in, and how APR/vibe-planning should consume Oracle outputs.

## Future-Self Notes
This captures the fourth integration pass principle: vibe-planning orchestrates at runtime, but a separate integration pass reconciles package implementations before release.

title: Guarantee dry-run and deterministic automation output
description:
## Background
Ensure all new commands that can spend money, call live providers, mutate browser state, acquire leases, write evidence, or touch remote browsers support --dry-run or an explicit harmless preview mode. Output must be deterministic under CI, NO_COLOR, and non-TTY.

## Design Notes
Dry-runs should show planned browser route, remote/local mode, provider locks, evidence path, prompt hash plan, paid/live call status, and recovery commands without submitting prompts or requiring confirmation.

## Acceptance Criteria

## Future-Self Notes
This keeps robot runs predictable and prevents accidental paid or browser-mutating work during planning.

title: Add privacy and secret-leak regression tests
description:
## Background
Add tests or snapshot guards proving normal evidence, logs, JSON envelopes, session metadata, remote endpoint data, and artifact indexes never include cookies, account email, auth headers, raw DOM, screenshots, raw private prompt text, raw private output, or plaintext remote tokens.

Include unsafe evidence mode tests proving quarantine and explicit opt-in. Use representative fake secrets and assert they do not appear in stdout/stderr/files.

title: Integrate leases into browser provider execution
description:
## Background
Wire lease acquisition and release into ChatGPT and Gemini browser runs. A protected browser run must acquire provider lock, verify browser/session/login/mode, submit prompt only after verification, capture output/evidence, and release lock on success, failure, timeout, or cancellation where safe.

## Design Notes
Use try/finally-style cleanup and session metadata so recover can identify abandoned runs. Do not let one provider corrupt the other provider's tab state.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests simulate success, provider failure, timeout, process interruption where practical, and verify locks are released or recoverable and evidence records the lease context.

## Future-Self Notes
This converts leases from a standalone diagnostic into a reliability primitive.

title: Gate unsafe evidence mode and quarantine artifacts
description:
## Background
Implement explicit unsafe evidence mode only for debugging, requiring a deliberate flag such as --evidence unsafe plus an acknowledgement flag. Unsafe mode must never be default and must be clearly marked as non-normal evidence.

## Design Notes
Unsafe screenshots/DOM/debug traces should be written to quarantine paths, excluded from normal artifact indexes and handoff packets, and never created by doctor/capabilities/dry-run commands.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests prove unsafe artifacts require both flags, are quarantined, are not referenced from normal artifact indexes, and secret scanning/redaction checks still run.

## Future-Self Notes
This prevents evidence from becoming a privacy leak while preserving a controlled debugging escape hatch.

title: Make remote browser the first-class Oracle browser path
description:
## Background
Remote browser epic. The workflow expects a remote authenticated browser endpoint as the primary path for balanced/audit planning. Local browser remains useful, but remote browser must be reliable, diagnosed, and safe under long Pro runs.

## Design Notes
Implement ORACLE_REMOTE_HOST and ORACLE_REMOTE_TOKEN support, --remote-browser preferred|required|off semantics, token redaction, remote doctor/status/attach commands, health and version reporting, and reconnect/heartbeat behavior. Interpret one shared profile as one logical user-authenticated browser identity boundary with provider locks, not necessarily a single physical Chrome directory.

## Success Criteria
Remote doctor, status, attach, browser lease planning, and long-run heartbeat/reconnect tests pass without printing token values or account identifiers.

## Future-Self Notes
This directly addresses failure mode FM-002: remote browser state is shared infrastructure, not a stateless API call.

title: Run release readiness verification for v18 Oracle work
description:
## Background
Before closing the root epic, run the repo's standard verification for substantive TypeScript changes plus the new v18 checks. At minimum: pnpm run format:check, pnpm run lint or pnpm run typecheck as appropriate, pnpm test or targeted vitest, contract fixture checks, and relevant manual/live smokes only when explicitly enabled and credentials/browser access are available.

## Design Notes
Respect AGENTS.md live-test constraints and do not run long paid/browser smokes by accident. If browser/serve paths changed, skim docs/manual-tests.md and rerun relevant manual smokes. Record any tests not run and why.

## Acceptance Criteria
Verification results are documented in the closing comment/release notes; no known dependency cycles remain in beads; br sync --flush-only has been run.

## Future-Self Notes
This is the final quality gate for the bead graph and implementation work.

title: Harden ChatGPT Pro browser formal-plan and synthesis routes
description:
## Background
ChatGPT route epic. Balanced/audit formal first plan and later synthesis/revision slots must run through ChatGPT Pro/latest Pro in the browser with the highest visible thinking effort, preferably remote, with evidence verified before prompt submission.

## Design Notes
Do not hard-code Heavy as the contract. Use ranked selector strategy: select Pro, enumerate visible effort controls if exposed, pick highest visible option, record observed label/rank/available-label hash, and block with typed errors on UI drift. Never click or auto-click ChatGPT Answer now during Pro thinking.

## Success Criteria
ChatGPT doctor, lease, and run paths can verify Pro/highest-visible effort; prompt submission is illegal before same-session verification; long Pro runs reconnect or return recoverable background state without false success.

## Future-Self Notes
This route is a protected workflow slot. Direct OpenAI API output must never satisfy chatgpt_pro_first_plan or chatgpt_pro_synthesis for vibe-planning.

title: Oracle vNext v18 browser/provider reliability implementation plan
description:
## Background
Root epic for translating PLAN/oracle-vnext-plan-bundle-v18.0.0 into executable Oracle work. The plan is v18.0.0 and targets browser/provider reliability for the Agent Flywheel planning substrate while keeping Oracle a general-purpose CLI. Oracle owns browser mechanics, remote browser transport, provider mode selection, output capture, redacted evidence, capability probes, leases, and robot-friendly JSON. Oracle does not own APR route policy, plan comparison, Plan IR, synthesis policy, or vibe-planning user coaching.

## Design Notes
Use the v18 bundle as the source of truth but do not require future implementers to reopen it. Keep protected routes explicit: ChatGPT Pro first plan and synthesis are Oracle browser routes; Gemini Deep Think is an Oracle browser route; Claude Code, xAI, and DeepSeek belong to their own access paths. Preserve the existing TypeScript CLI shape and harden existing browser, gemini-web, remote, session, MCP, and CLI surfaces instead of replacing the browser stack.

## Success Criteria
All child epics are closed; no dependency cycles exist; br dep cycles is empty; contract, mock, browser, and live-gated validation beads are represented; downstream implementers can understand scope, non-goals, acceptance criteria, and order from beads alone.

## Future-Self Notes
Root AGENTS.md precedence was observed. Local PLAN validators passed before bead creation: validate-subset and contract-fixture-smoke both returned ok. Existing repo appears to be a TypeScript pnpm CLI at version 0.11.1 with browser, gemini-web, remote, MCP, and live-test surfaces already present.

title: Add package scripts for v18 validation and logged E2E matrix
description:
## Background
Add package-level validation scripts for the v18 work so future agents and CI can run the intended test matrix without rediscovering individual vitest files or ad hoc commands. The plan asks for comprehensive unit tests and E2E scripts with detailed logging; this bead makes the script surface explicit.

## Design Notes
Add or update package.json scripts after the underlying tests exist. Suggested shape: test:v18:unit for contract/envelope/evidence/lease/remote unit tests, test:v18:fixtures for browser selector and fixture tests, test:v18:e2e:mock for the no-live-provider rehearsal, test:v18:privacy for secret/redaction assertions, and test:v18:live for opt-in live browser smokes. Each script should either emit or point to the structured test artifact directory defined by the logging-standard bead. Live scripts must remain opt-in and respect ORACLE_LIVE_TEST/ORACLE_LIVE_BROWSER gating.

## Acceptance Criteria
package.json exposes documented v18 test scripts or equivalent repo-standard commands; scripts run deterministically in CI when live flags are absent; logged E2E artifacts are discoverable; release verification and manual docs reference the same script names; no script runs paid/browser live work unless explicit live env flags are set.

## Future-Self Notes
This keeps verification boring. A future agent should be able to run one or two named commands and get structured logs instead of reverse-engineering the test layout.

description:

title: Build the synchronous browser lease coordinator
description:
## Background
Browser lease epic. v18 narrows the old scheduler idea into a synchronous lease coordinator that protects a shared authenticated browser profile from concurrent ChatGPT/Gemini tab and state corruption.

## Design Notes
Use provider-scoped locks for the shared logical profile. The coordinator must acquire a lock, verify reachability/login/mode, allow prompt submission only after verification, capture output/evidence, release on success or failure, and return typed recovery when blocked. Do not build a daemon, queue service, distributed scheduler, or background worker.

## Success Criteria
browser leases plan/status/acquire/release/recover work in JSON; stale locks and timeouts are visible and recoverable; browser runs integrate lease acquisition and release. Unit and concurrency tests cover acquisition, release, stale/corrupt locks, provider isolation, timeout recovery, cross-platform paths, and detailed structured logs for lease state transitions.

## Future-Self Notes
This is intentionally small. If future work wants scheduling, it belongs in APR or a later proven need, not in this v18 Oracle scope.

title: Add structured logging and artifact capture standards for v18 tests
description:
## Background
Define a reusable testing standard for v18 unit, fixture, mock E2E, and opt-in live tests so failures are explainable from logs and saved artifacts rather than requiring a debugger. The current graph has broad test beads; this bead makes the logging expectations explicit and shared.

## Design Notes
For each v18 test layer, require structured step logs with run_id, provider slot, command under test, dry-run/live flag, remote/local browser mode, lease id when relevant, evidence id when relevant, elapsed time, retry/recover command, and redaction assertions. For E2E and live tests, save a compact artifact packet containing JSON envelopes, sanitized stdout/stderr, evidence verification output, and a per-step summary. This standard should be available early after the JSON envelope helper; product run_progress implementation can then reuse it where helpful, but test logging must not wait for full browser progress implementation. Never store secrets, raw DOM, screenshots, cookies, raw private prompt text, raw private output, or hidden reasoning text in default artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
A shared test helper or documented convention exists; unit/browser/E2E/live test beads reference it; at least one mock E2E test demonstrates detailed logs and sanitized artifacts; privacy tests assert the logging artifacts are redacted.

## Future-Self Notes
This is not extra ceremony. It is what lets a future agent know whether a failure is a schema mismatch, selector drift, lease conflict, remote auth issue, empty output capture, or a legitimate long Pro wait. It should become ready shortly after the envelope helper, before the heavier browser routes are complete.

title: Represent one shared logical browser profile with provider locks
description:
## Background
Implement the v18 interpretation of one shared profile as a shared logical user-authenticated browser identity boundary. Expose one profile_id_hash/auth_profile_id_hash and provider locks for chatgpt and gemini, while allowing internal provider-specific technical profiles if needed by existing Oracle implementation.

## Design Notes
APR and vibe-planning must only see endpoint, profile hash, and locks, never cookies, DOM, screenshots, raw profile paths, or account identifiers. Internal implementation details stay inside Oracle.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests prove endpoint/profile metadata is stable and redacted, and that ChatGPT/Gemini locks are visible as browser:shared-profile:chatgpt and browser:shared-profile:gemini or documented equivalents.

## Future-Self Notes
This resolves the plan nuance that the product requirement is identity/coordination, not a brittle Chrome directory constraint.

title: Surface non-Oracle provider boundaries and API substitution guards
description:
## Background
Represent Claude Code, xAI/Grok, and DeepSeek V4 Pro Reasoning + Search as workflow routes without making Oracle implement APR's adapters. Claude uses Claude Code CLI/subscription, xAI uses xAI API, and DeepSeek uses official DeepSeek API with thinking enabled, reasoning_effort=max, and APR search tool calls.

## Design Notes
Oracle capabilities/provider metadata may state which routes are outside Oracle ownership and which API routes are allowed. Do not use this as permission to substitute APIs for ChatGPT, Gemini, or Claude protected workflow slots.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover capabilities metadata, DeepSeek route marked not Oracle-owned for this workflow, xAI/DeepSeek API allowed only for their slots, and protected slot API substitutions rejected.

## Future-Self Notes
This lets APR build its own adapters while still seeing a coherent provider matrix.

title: Expose TOON prompt-block passthrough without owning toon_rust
description:
## Background
Implement Oracle behavior for optional TOON context compression as passthrough only. Oracle should advertise prompt_payload_format_passthrough/toon_prompt_blocks_passthrough, hash prompt bytes, and never decode, validate, vendor, install, execute, benchmark, or require toon_rust as part of browser automation.

## Design Notes
If prompt text contains TOON blocks, Oracle sends it like any other prompt text and records hashes. Canonical artifacts remain JSON/Markdown. JSON CLI output remains JSON. TOON availability must not affect browser readiness.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover prompt passthrough, no toon/tru command invocation, no dependency on toon_rust, context serialization fixture compatibility, and JSON fallback warning semantics where Oracle surfaces policy metadata.

## Future-Self Notes
This honors the license gate and package boundary: APR/vibe-planning own context serialization decisions.

title: Define evidence storage, artifact index, and redaction defaults
description:
## Background
Implement where redacted evidence ledgers are written, how they are referenced from sessions/artifact indexes, and how redaction defaults are enforced. Evidence for critical browser slots must be findable but private by default.

## Design Notes
Store JSON evidence with stable IDs and hashes. Normal artifact indexes may reference redacted evidence paths and hashes. Unsafe artifacts, if ever requested, go to quarantine and are excluded from normal APR/handoff indexes.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover evidence path creation, artifact index entries, default redacted mode, and absence of forbidden fields: cookies, account email, raw DOM, screenshots, auth headers, raw private prompt/output.

## Future-Self Notes
This is the privacy-preserving evidence foundation.

title: Map existing Oracle code paths to v18 responsibilities
description:
## Background
Create an implementation map from existing Oracle TypeScript modules to v18 responsibilities: CLI command registration, browser ChatGPT automation, gemini-web automation, remote service/client, session storage, heartbeat/logging, MCP surfaces, live tests, and docs/manual tests. This map is the first ready task because it keeps the rest of the graph grounded in the repo that exists today.

## Design Notes
Start by reading src/oracle.ts, src/cli/options.ts, src/browser/*, src/gemini-web/*, src/remote/*, src/session*.ts, src/heartbeat.ts, src/mcp/*, docs/manual-tests.md, and relevant tests. Identify what can be hardened in place and what truly requires new modules. Avoid replacing the browser stack. Include an implementation-slicing table that names likely file ownership boundaries for contracts/envelopes, remote browser, browser leases, evidence, ChatGPT selectors/state, Gemini selectors/state, provider-result normalization, MCP, tests, and docs. Include suggested Agent Mail reservation globs for each track so parallel implementers can reserve files by bead ID before editing.

## Acceptance Criteria
A short internal implementation note or bead comment identifies target files/modules for each major v18 surface, recommended implementation order, file ownership/reservation guidance, and any areas where current code already satisfies part of v18. No product code changes are required by this bead unless a local convention already has an architecture note location.

## Future-Self Notes
This should prevent duplicate abstractions, reduce merge conflicts, and keep implementation sympathetic to the current repo. It should also remind future agents that root AGENTS.md forbids deleting files and script-based code rewrites, so large mechanical changes need careful manual slicing or disjoint worker ownership.

title: Harden Gemini Deep Think browser route and output capture
description:
## Background
Gemini route epic. Gemini 3.1 Pro Deep Think must run through Oracle browser automation with Deep Think selected and verified before prompt submission. If a separate thinking-level control appears, select high and record it.

## Design Notes
Build on the existing gemini-web code, but harden selection evidence and output parsing. The output parser must not stop at an empty placeholder chunk if later stream chunks contain the real answer.

## Success Criteria
Gemini doctor, lease, and run paths can verify Deep Think/high-if-exposed; prompt submission is illegal before verification; provider results link evidence and never mark empty or unverified captures as success. Unit tests cover state transitions and parser behavior; browser fixture tests cover selector drift and Deep Think controls; mock/live-gated E2E tests verify evidence linkage, non-empty output capture, structured logs, and no Gemini API substitution.

## Future-Self Notes
This protects the required independent reviewer slot. Gemini API must not substitute for Gemini Deep Think in balanced/audit planning.

title: Add a grouped changelog entry for user-visible v18 behavior
description:
## Background
If implementation changes end-user behavior, update the changelog with one grouped entry covering remote browser hardening, leases, evidence, doctors, protected provider verification, and live smoke guidance.

## Design Notes
Before editing, read the top roughly 100 lines of the changelog and follow existing style. Do not scatter multiple unrelated bullets across the file.

## Acceptance Criteria
Changelog accurately summarizes user-visible changes and avoids over-claiming cryptographic proof or hidden provider backend verification.

## Future-Self Notes
AGENTS.md says to ask whether a feature matters to end users; for this v18 work it almost certainly does, so this bead should remain until release prep confirms the changelog decision.
